meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for unique — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, use optional preconditions/proofShape/falsePositiveSignals/reportEvidence when present, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: Vulnerable Code Pattern
cards.detect: // ❌ CRITICAL: No access control on callback
check: VERIFY: // CRITICAL: No access control on callback
PROVE: TransferHelper.safeTransferFrom(token1, source, address(pool), amount1Owed);
cards.antipattern: TransferHelper.safeTransferFrom(token1, source, address(pool), amount1Owed);
cards.validWhen: // CRITICAL: No access control on callback
cards.proofShape: TransferHelper.safeTransferFrom(token1, source, address(pool), amount1Owed);

cards.title: Compiler-Level Vulnerability Patterns
cards.detect: The Vyper compiler versions 0.2.15, 0.2.16, and 0.3.0 had a bug in the code generation for the `@nonreentrant` decorator.
check: VERIFY: Vyper source was compiled with vulnerable versions 0.2.15, 0.2.16, or 0.3.0 and relies on @nonreentrant for cross-function protection.; A...
PROVE: Attacker calls remove_liquidity() on the Curve pool (compiled with vulnerable Vyper); Pool sends ETH to the attacker before updating state (standard ETH transfer)
FALSIFY: Not this bug for Solidity ReentrancyGuard or Vyper versions outside the affected compiler range unless a separate compiler issue is proven.; Safe if vulnerable compiler...
cards.validWhen: Vyper source was compiled with vulnerable versions 0.2.15, 0.2.16, or 0.3.0 and relies on @nonreentrant for cross-function protection.; A liquidity/removal/withdraw path...
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug for Solidity ReentrancyGuard or Vyper versions outside the affected compiler range unless a separate compiler issue is proven.; Safe if vulnerable compiler...
preconditions: deployed bytecode compiled by the affected compiler; source-only pattern matching is insufficient.
Attacker calls remove_liquidity() on the Curve pool (compiled with vulnerable Vyper)
Pool sends ETH to the attacker before updating state (standard ETH transfer)
Attacker's receive() function calls add_liquidity() on the same pool
Both calls complete — attacker holds more LP tokens than they should
cards.proofShape: Attacker calls remove_liquidity() on the Curve pool (compiled with vulnerable Vyper) -> Pool sends ETH to the attacker before updating state (standard ETH transfer) -> Attacker's receive() function calls add_liquidity()...
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug for Solidity ReentrancyGuard or Vyper versions outside the affected compiler range unless a separate compiler issue is proven.
Safe if vulnerable compiler output is not deployed, or no external callback path can reach another state-changing guarded function.
Requires deployed bytecode compiled by the affected compiler; source-only pattern matching is insufficient.

cards.title: 1. Transient Storage (EIP-1153) Authorization Bypass via CREATE2
cards.detect: EIP-1153 introduced `tstore`/`tload` opcodes for transient storage that persists only within a single transaction.
check: VERIFY: EIP-1153 introduced tstore/tload opcodes for transient storage that persists only within a single transaction.
PROVE: Create fake ERC20 tokens (AttackerC_A, AttackerC_B) with malicious mint() that returns uint160(address(this)); Create a fake Uniswap V3 pool using these tokens
ANTIPATTERN: Transient storage used for callback authorization
cards.antipattern: Transient storage used for callback authorization
cards.validWhen: EIP-1153 introduced tstore/tload opcodes for transient storage that persists only within a single transaction.
preconditions: Create fake ERC20 tokens (AttackerC_A, AttackerC_B) with malicious mint() that returns uint160(address(this))
Initialize the victim vault with the fake token pair
Call vault.mint() — the fake token's mint() returns attacker's address, which gets stored via tstore(1, amount)
The deployed contract calls vault.uniswapV3SwapCallback() — passes tload(1) check
Drain all vault tokens through the authorized callback
cards.proofShape: Create fake ERC20 tokens (AttackerC_A, AttackerC_B) with malicious mint() that returns uint160(address(this)) -> Create a fake Uniswap V3 pool using these tokens -> Initialize the victim vault with the fake token pair

cards.title: 2. Fee-On-Transfer Token Overcharge + AMM Sync Drain
cards.detect: When a token's `_transfer()` function applies fees based on a percentage list where `sum(shares) > 100%`, the sender is...
check: VERIFY: When a token's _transfer() function applies fees based on a percentage list where sum(shares) > 100%, the sender is debited more tokens than the...
PROVE: Flash loan USDT; Buy the buggy token from the pair (large amount)
ANTIPATTERN: transactionFee() splits fees by unbounded percentages
cards.antipattern: transactionFee() splits fees by unbounded percentages
cards.validWhen: When a token's _transfer() function applies fees based on a percentage list where sum(shares) > 100%, the sender is debited more tokens than the recipient receives.
preconditions: a token's _transfer() function applies fees based on a percentage list where sum(shares) > 100%, the sender is debited more...
Call pair.skim(attacker) — extract excess tokens (fee triggers again, debiting pair more)
cards.proofShape: Flash loan USDT -> Buy the buggy token from the pair (large amount) -> Transfer tokens TO the pair — fee overcharge debits pair's balance excessively

preconditions: a staking contract calculates rewards per-address without tracking total claimed amounts globally, an attacker can transfer...

cards.title: 4. Batch Array Refund Multiplication
cards.detect: When a sale/purchase function accepts arrays of recipients, rates, and refund flags, and processes refunds...
check: VERIFY: When a sale/purchase function accepts arrays of recipients, rates, and refund flags, and processes refunds per-iteration using msg.value (which is...
PROVE: Calculate: refundPerIteration = msg.value / rate (e.g., 1 ETH / 10 = 0.1 ETH); Calculate: maxIterations = contractBalance / refundPerIteration (e.g., 20)
ANTIPATTERN: buy() processes refunds per-array-element using msg.value
cards.antipattern: buy() processes refunds per-array-element using msg.value
cards.validWhen: When a sale/purchase function accepts arrays of recipients, rates, and refund flags, and processes refunds per-iteration using msg.value (which is constant across all...
preconditions: a sale/purchase function accepts arrays of recipients, rates, and refund flags, and processes refunds per-iteration using...
Build arrays: all recipients = attacker, all refundFlags = true
cards.proofShape: Calculate: refundPerIteration = msg.value / rate (e.g., 1 ETH / 10 = 0.1 ETH) -> Calculate: maxIterations = contractBalance / refundPerIteration (e.g., 20) -> Build arrays: all recipients = attacker, all refundFlags =...

cards.title: 5. Bonding Curve Arithmetic Overflow in Price Calculation
cards.detect: Bonding curve contracts calculate buy/sell prices using polynomial formulas.
check: VERIFY: Bonding curve contracts calculate buy/sell prices using polynomial formulas.
PROVE: Read bonding curve parameters: reserve, THETA, totalSupply; Solve: $\text{amount} = \sqrt{\frac{\text{type(uint256).max}}{100 \times \text{reserve}} +...
ANTIPATTERN: Bonding curve price formula overflows at extreme amounts
cards.antipattern: Bonding curve price formula overflows at extreme amounts
cards.validWhen: Bonding curve contracts calculate buy/sell prices using polynomial formulas.
preconditions: Read bonding curve parameters: reserve, THETA, totalSupply
Solve: $\text{amount} = \sqrt{\frac{\text{type(uint256).max}}{100 \times \text{reserve}} + \text{totalSupply}^2} -...
Repeat until pool drained (each iteration extracts more ETH than deposited)
cards.proofShape: Read bonding curve parameters: reserve, THETA, totalSupply -> Solve: $\text{amount} = \sqrt{\frac{\text{type(uint256).max}}{100 \times \text{reserve}} + \text{totalSupply}^2} -...

cards.title: ConvertToShares Manipulation: Blocking Deposits via Direct Token Transfers
cards.detect: ERC4626 vaults typically calculate total assets using:
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: Unbounded loop over user-controlled array can exceed block gas limit; Signal 2: External call failure causes entire transaction to revert
PROVE: balanceOf can be manipulated by direct transfers
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Loop iterations are bounded by a reasonable constant; Safe if: External call failures are handled gracefully (try/catch or pull pattern)
IMPACT: Zero Share Minting: Deposits return 0 shares, users lose funds
LOOK FOR: _totalAssets() using asset.balanceOf(address(this))
cards.antipattern: balanceOf can be manipulated by direct transfers
cards.securePattern: Permanent dead shares prevent empty vault manipulation
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Unbounded loop over user-controlled array can exceed block gas limit; Signal 2: External call failure causes entire transaction to revert
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Loop iterations are bounded by a reasonable constant; Safe if: External call failures are handled gracefully (try/catch or pull pattern)
cards.impact: Zero Share Minting: Deposits return 0 shares, users lose funds
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
totalSupply > 0 but small
cards.proofShape: balanceOf can be manipulated by direct transfers
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Loop iterations are bounded by a reasonable constant
Safe if: External call failures are handled gracefully (try/catch or pull pattern)
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.title: Cross-Contract Reentrancy Allows YIELD_TOKEN Theft in Multi-Vault ERC4626 Systems
cards.detect: The `WithdrawalRequestManager.stakeTokens()` function captures the initial `YIELD_TOKEN` balance **before** executing a...
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: External call (.call, .transfer, token transfer) occurs before state variable update; Signal 2: Token implements callback hooks (ERC-777,...
PROVE: *Prerequisites; WithdrawalRequestManager manages multiple vault strategies
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Contract uses ReentrancyGuard (nonReentrant) on all entry points; Safe if: All state updates complete before any external call (strict CEI)
IMPACT: Inflated Share Minting: Attacker receives more vault shares than their deposit value
LOOK FOR: Balance snapshot before external calls: balanceOf() ... externalCall() ... balanceOf()
cards.antipattern: Only validates first and last tokens, not middle path
cards.securePattern: Balance snapshot after all external calls
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: External call (.call, .transfer, token transfer) occurs before state variable update; Signal 2: Token implements callback hooks (ERC-777, ERC-721) and protocol...
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Contract uses ReentrancyGuard (nonReentrant) on all entry points; Safe if: All state updates complete before any external call (strict CEI)
cards.impact: Inflated Share Minting: Attacker receives more vault shares than their deposit value
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
WithdrawalRequestManager manages multiple vault strategies
control a vault that is whitelisted OR exploit via EIP-7702
Attacker can control a vault that is whitelisted OR exploit via EIP-7702
Attacker identifies target: Pending withdrawal requests hold YIELD_TOKEN in the manager
cards.proofShape: *Prerequisites -> WithdrawalRequestManager manages multiple vault strategies -> Other users have pending withdrawal requests (yield tokens sitting in manager)
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Contract uses ReentrancyGuard (nonReentrant) on all entry points
Safe if: All state updates complete before any external call (strict CEI)
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.title: Flash Loan Vault Deflation Attack: Draining Accumulated Yield via Share Price Reset
cards.detect: Most ERC4626 implementations calculate share price using:
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity...
PROVE: *Prerequisites; ERC4626 IBT vault with accumulated yield (share price > 1)
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
IMPACT: Share Price Reset: Vault's share price forcibly returned to default value
Does the protocol offer flash loans of ERC4626 shares?
Can the flash loan drain the entire underlying vault?
cards.antipattern: Allows borrowing entire IBT supply without rate protection
cards.securePattern: Prevent borrowing amounts that could empty underlying vault
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: Critical input parameter not validated against expected range or format; Signal 2: Oracle data consumed without staleness check or sanity bounds
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller; Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
cards.impact: Share Price Reset: Vault's share price forcibly returned to default value
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
totalAssets = 0 and totalShares = 0, the vault falls back to a default price (typically 1:1).
ERC4626 IBT vault with accumulated yield (share price > 1)
PrincipalToken contract holds majority/all of IBT supply
cards.proofShape: *Prerequisites -> ERC4626 IBT vault with accumulated yield (share price > 1) -> PrincipalToken contract holds majority/all of IBT supply
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Validation exists but is in an upstream function caller
Safe if: Parameter range is inherently bounded by the type or protocol invariant
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
